skill.name: scroll-experience
skill.description: Expert guidance for building immersive scroll-driven web experiences, including parallax storytelling, scroll-triggered animation, sticky sections, and cinematic page flow.
skill.summary: Design scroll-driven stories with parallax, sticky sections, reveals, and accessible motion patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown fences, inline code formatting, frontend examples, and prose in SKILL.md. No executable shell usage, Ruby backtick execution, cryptographic operations, filesystem access, network access, or prompt injection text was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged external command locations are Markdown code fences, JavaScript and CSS frontend examples, or inline skill names. No Ruby backtick execution, shell command execution, or user-controlled command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown delimiters, client-side animation snippets, or inline references. There is no shell, Ruby, or command execution context in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Plain Text
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations contain skill description text, design guidance, a sharp edge table item, and related skill names. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, credential handling, or security-sensitive code appears in these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact flagged lines are natural language or inline Markdown references. No crypto API, hash operation, or weak algorithm use was found.

content.value_statement: Static pages can feel flat when a story needs pacing, depth, or guided attention. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan scroll animation systems with accessible, performance-aware patterns.
actual_capabilities: Compare common scroll animation libraries for different frontend needs.
Plan parallax layers, story beats, sticky sections, and reveal sequences.
Suggest GSAP, Framer Motion, and CSS scroll-timeline implementation patterns.
Identify scroll design anti-patterns such as hijacking and animation overload.
Recommend mobile, accessibility, and performance safeguards for scroll effects.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not installed dependencies.
It does not deliver a complete runnable app by itself.
Browser behavior and device performance still need hands-on testing.
Examples focus on frontend experience design, not backend architecture.

use_cases.title: Product Launch Story
use_cases.description: Plan a product page where features appear through pinned sections, reveal moments, and measured parallax depth.

use_cases.title: Interactive Editorial
use_cases.description: Structure a long-form story with scroll-driven visuals, progress cues, and accessible fallback content.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Case Study
use_cases.description: Create a case study flow that uses sticky comparisons, image transitions, and restrained motion.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Scroll Stack
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new page
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a scroll animation approach for a responsive marketing page. Compare GSAP, Framer Motion, Lenis, and native CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Map Story Beats
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this page concept into five scroll story beats. Include visual changes, text reveals, and mobile fallbacks for each beat.

prompt_templates.title: Design Sticky Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan sticky sections for a feature walkthrough. Define what stays pinned, what moves, and how the experience should degrade on phones.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Motion Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this scroll experience plan for accessibility, scroll hijacking, animation overload, content visibility, and performance risks.

output_examples.input: Plan a scroll story for a new analytics dashboard page.
output: A five-part page arc with a full-viewport hook, context section, pinned feature walkthrough, comparison moment, and final call to action.
A motion plan that uses subtle reveals for text and stronger animation only for key dashboard transitions.
Mobile notes that replace heavy parallax with simpler fades and natural scrolling.

output_examples.input: Help me avoid bad scroll animation patterns.
output: A checklist covering natural scroll behavior, reduced motion, visible critical content, stable layout, and measured animation density.
Specific warnings against scroll hijacking, desktop-only effects, and excessive simultaneous movement.

best_practices: Start with the content story, then add motion only where it improves clarity.
Keep natural scrolling and provide reduced-motion alternatives.
Test scroll performance on mobile devices before launch.
anti_patterns: Do not hijack scroll speed or block normal browser navigation.
Do not hide important content behind complex animation timing.
Do not design effects that only work on large desktop screens.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps plan scroll-driven web experiences with parallax, sticky sections, reveal animations, and narrative pacing.

faq.question: Does it require GSAP?
faq.answer: No. It discusses GSAP, Framer Motion, Lenis, Locomotive Scroll, and native CSS options.

faq.question: Can it write implementation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes frontend examples and can guide implementation choices for common scroll patterns.

faq.question: Is it suitable for mobile design?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes mobile-first planning, simpler effects on phones, and graceful degradation.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It warns against inaccessible scroll experiences and supports reduced motion and content-first design.

faq.question: Can it replace browser testing?
faq.answer: No. Scroll performance, layout stability, and touch behavior still need real browser and device testing.
